meta.title: Identification of surface-exposed Yersinia pestis proteins by radio-iodination and biotinylation

abstractHtml: When whole cells (stationary phase) of Yersinia pestis strain EV76 were radiolabelled with Iodogen and 131I, 16 major and 10 minor surface-exposed outer membrane proteins (OMPs) were identified. Labelling with N-hydroxysuccinimidyl 6-biotinylamino-hexanoate (biotin X-NHS) resulted in a complex protein profile detectable after blotting and developing with peroxidase-conjugated avidin. Y. pestis cell fractionation revealed that biotin X-NHS labelled not only OMPs but also proteins of inner cell compartments. Therefore, radiolabelling was the more reliable technique for identifying the OMPs of Y. pestis.
